_doc: Tier model for the 10-language PE benchmark suite. Single source of truth for which langs are in scope for which problem ranges. Consumed by coverage_report.py, three_metric_report.py, compare_languages.py, final_analysis.py via scripts/tiers.py. See project_pe_tier_model_2026-05-22 in auto-memory for rationale.

tier_1_foundation.label: Foundation
tier_1_foundation.description: All 10 languages; the apples-to-apples cross-language comparison surface.

tier_2_deep_coverage.label: Deep Coverage
tier_2_deep_coverage.description: Languages we've intentionally pushed past 200; richer comparison among the deeper five. Rust promoted 2026-05-25 — replaces Zig as the user-auditable native lang for long-term maintenance, while keeping Zig in for compiled-lang diversity.

tier_3_frontier.label: Frontier
tier_3_frontier.description: C++ and Go paired on unexplored problems. Go replaces Python as C++'s exploration partner (decided 2026-05-22 — Python's wall cost made it impractical above level 4).

_historical_exceptions_note: Languages may have committed solutions beyond their highest tier's problem_range (e.g., Rust has 36 problems above 200). Those stay preserved per the historical-exception convention in project_200_cap_policy auto-memory, but do NOT count for tier-comparison stats. Reports show them only in the lang's own data, not in cross-tier rankings.
